text: Contains functions regarding the physics engine.
Collision filter types
@table
dynamicBody
staticBody
character
areaTrigger
joints
terrainSurface
terrainAsset
harvestable
areaTrigger
static
default
all
allTerrain
@table
Physics types are used to define an object's characteristics is in the physics world. Upon a raycast or collision detection, these types are used to find out what object was intersected.
@table
"invalid";			No object.
"terrainSurface";		The ground.
"terrainAsset";		Trees and boulders.
"lift";				A [Lift].
"body";				A [Body].
"character";			A [Character].
"joint";				A [Joint].
"harvestable";		A [Harvestable].
"vision";				A collision area used by sensors.
@table
Applies an impulse to a [Shape], changing its velocity immediately. The impulse is applied to the shape's centerpoint with an optional offset.
params: The object on which the impulse is exerted on.
The direction and strength of the impulse.
Whether the impulse is applied in world space coordinates. (Defaults to local rotation)
The offset from the center point. (Defaults to no offset)

text: Applies an impulse to a [Body], changing its velocity immediately. The impulse is applied to the body's center of mass with an optional offset.
params: The object on which the impulse is exerted on.
The direction and strength of the impulse.
Whether the impulse is applied in world space coordinates. (Defaults to local rotation)
The offset from the center point. (Defaults to no offset)

text: Applies an impulse to a [Character], changing its velocity immediately. The impulse is applied to the character's centerpoint.
params: The character on which the impulse is exerted on.
The direction and strength of the impulse.

text: Applies a torque impulse to a [Body], changing its angular velocity immediately. The torque is applied along the body's center of mass, making it rotate.
params: The object on which the torque is exerted on.
The direction and strength of the torque.
Whether the torque is applied in world space coordinates. (Defaults to local rotation)

text: Returns the number of collision objects that are found inside a given capsule.
The capsule is formed by connecting two spheres.
params: The world position of the first sphere.
The world position of the second sphere.
The collision mask. Defaults to [sm.physics.filter, sm.physics.filter.default] (Optional)

text: Returns true if any collision object is found inside a given capsule.
Faster than capsuleContactCount when only checking for existence.
The capsule is formed by connecting two spheres.
params: The world position of the first sphere.
The world position of the second sphere.
The collision mask. Defaults to [sm.physics.filter, sm.physics.filter.default] (Optional)
return: True if any contact was found.

text: Performs a capsule ray cast (Z-axis aligned) between two positions.
The returned [RaycastResult] contains information about any object intersected by the ray.
If the ray cast is called from within a shape (e.g. a Sensor), a [Body] may be provided which the ray will not intersect.
params: The height of the cylindrical part of the capsule.
The collision mask. Defaults to [sm.physics.filter, sm.physics.filter.default] (Optional)
The world to perform the cast in. (Optional)
A table of uuids {[Uuid], ...} of [Shape], [Harvestable] or [Character] type to be ignored. (Optional)
return: True if raycast was successful; The raycast result data.

text: Performs a distance ray cast from a position with a given direction.
@note

[sm.physics.distanceRaycast] is generally cheaper to use than [sm.physics.raycast] as it performs collision checks in a simplified world. If the raycast is only used for checking collision, it is advised to use this method instead.
@note
return: 2 values: whether raycast was successful; the fraction (0–1) of the distance reached until collision divided by the ray's length.

text: Creates an explosion at given position. The explosion creates a shockwave that is capable of destroying blocks and pushing characters and creations away.
Shapes that are within the explosion's destruction radius may receive the event [ShapeClass.server_onExplosion, server_onExplosionHit].
@note
The destruction level is the damage effect on blocks and parts, determining how likely it is that they are destroyed. This is related to the `qualityLevel` found in parts json-files.
Any quality level equal to or less than the destruction level may be destroyed. The effect fades one level every half travelled of the remaining destruction radius.
A quality level of 0 means a block or part is indestructible.
@note
params: The center point of the explosion.
The destruction level affecting nearby objects.
The destruction radius. Objects inside this sphere may be destroyed.
The impulse radius. Objects inside this sphere are affected by an [sm.physics.applyImpulse, impulse].
The impulse strength of the explosion. The strength diminishes with distance.
The name of the effect to be played upon explosion. (Optional)
The table containing the parameters for the effect. (Optional)
The world to cause the explosion in. (Optional)
The damage of the explosion. Defaults to 2 * destruction level (Optional)
The source of the explosion. Defaults to none (Optional)
The explosionType. Defaults to nil uuid (Optional)

text: Returns a table of the game objects that are found inside the given box
params: The world position of the box center.
The half extents of the box.
The collision mask. Defaults to [sm.physics.filter, sm.physics.filter.static] and [sm.physics.filter, sm.physics.filter.dynamicBody] and [sm.physics.filter, sm.physics.filter.character] (Optional)
return: The table with tables of objects found inside the box. { bodies={[Body], ..}, characters={[Character], ..}, harvestables={[Harvestable], ..}, lifts={[Lift], ..} }

text: Returns the gravitational acceleration affecting [Shape, shapes] and [Body, bodies].
Returns the material at the given position in the terrain.
params: The world position to check the material at.

text: Returns the material id from name
Returns a table of all shapes colliding with a given box.
params: The center position of the box.
The halved size of the box.
return: The table of found shapes. {{shape=[Shape], contactWorldPosition=[Vec3] }, ..}

text: Returns a table of all shapes colliding with a given sphere.
params: The center position of the sphere.
return: The table of found shapes. {{shape=[Shape], contactWorldPosition=[Vec3] }, ..}

text: Returns a table of the game objects that are found inside the given sphere
params: The world position of the sphere.
The world to search in. Defaults to current world (Optional)
The collision mask. Defaults to [sm.physics.filter, sm.physics.filter.static] and [sm.physics.filter, sm.physics.filter.dynamicBody] and [sm.physics.filter, sm.physics.filter.character] (Optional)
return: The table with tables of objects found inside the sphere. { bodies={[Body], ..}, characters={[Character], ..}, harvestables={[Harvestable], ..}, lifts={[Lift], ..} }

text: Returns whether a point is in a liquid or not.
return: Whether the point is inside liquid or not

text: Returns whether a sphere is hitting a liquid or not.
return: Whether the sphere is hitting a liquid or not

text: Performs multiple sphere and/or raycasts given a table of parameters.
Type can be "sphere" or "ray". Radius is ignored for rays.
ignoreUuids option is a table of uuids {[Uuid], ...} of [Shape], [Harvestable] or [Character] type to be ignored.
params: Table of casts. { type=string, startPoint=[Vec3], endPoint=[Vec3], radius=number, mask=[sm.physics.filter], ignoreUuids=table }
The world to perform the cast in. (Optional)
return: Array of pairs of boolean and [RaycastResult]. {{boolean,  [RaycastResult]}, ..}

text: Performs a ray cast between two positions.
The returned [RaycastResult] contains information about any object intersected by the ray.
If the ray cast is called from within a shape (e.g. a Sensor), a [Body] may be provided which the ray will not intersect.
params: The collision mask. Defaults to [sm.physics.filter, sm.physics.filter.default] (Optional)
The world to perform the raycast in. (Optional)
A table of uuids {[Uuid], ...} of [Shape], [Harvestable] or [Character] type to be ignored. (Optional)
return: True if raycast was successful; The raycast result data.

text: Performs a ray cast between two positions to find a specific target.
a [Body] must be provided as a target.
The returned [RaycastResult] contains information about any object intersected by the ray.
params: The body to be exclusively checked.
The world to perform the raycast in. (Optional)
A table of uuids {[Uuid], ...} of [Shape], [Harvestable] or [Character] type to be ignored. (Optional)
return: True if raycast was successful; The raycast result data.

text: Sets the gravitational acceleration affecting [Shape, shapes] and [Body, bodies].
Returns the number of collision objects that are found inside the given sphere
params: The world position of the sphere.
True if terrain should be included in the test (Defaults to false)
True if water should be included (Defaults to false)
The collision mask. Defaults to [sm.physics.filter, sm.physics.filter.default]. Using this will override "includeTerrain" and "countWater". (Optional)

text: Returns true if any collision object is found inside the given sphere.
Faster than sphereContactCount when only checking for existence.
params: The world position of the sphere.
True if terrain should be included in the test (Defaults to false)
True if water should be included (Defaults to false)
The collision mask. Defaults to [sm.physics.filter, sm.physics.filter.default]. Using this will override "includeTerrain" and "countWater". (Optional)
return: True if any contact was found.

text: Performs a spherical ray cast between two positions.
The returned [RaycastResult] contains information about any object intersected by the ray.
If the ray cast is called from within a shape (e.g. a Sensor), a [Body] may be provided which the ray will not intersect.
params: The collision mask. Defaults to [sm.physics.filter, sm.physics.filter.default] (Optional)
The world to perform the cast in. (Optional)
A table of uuids {[Uuid], ...} of [Shape], [Harvestable] or [Character] type to be ignored. (Optional)
return: True if raycast was successful; The raycast result data.
